era_context: Late Third Dynasty Egypt (Huni's final years); post-Akkad Mesopotamia. The Indus Valley Civilization was mature and extensive. Early Bronze Age cultures in the Mediterranean and Near East were increasingly interconnected through trade.

events.title: Huni's reign, Third Dynasty Egypt (final years)
events.description: Huni's reign approaches its end. No specific events are documented for this year. The transition to Sneferu and the Fourth Dynasty would occur within the next few years, marking a shift toward the monumental pyramid-building age. Huni's reign is estimated to have lasted approximately 24 years, though this figure is uncertain.
events.certainty_note: Huni's reign length is traditionally given as 24 years, but this is derived from later king lists with significant uncertainty (±5–10 years).

disconfirming_evidence: No disconfirming evidence identified for events listed.
historiographic_note: 2196 BCE falls within a period of minimal documentation. No contemporary sources record events for this year. The historical record is entirely dependent on later king lists and archaeological inference.

graph_edges.note: Huni's reign ends; Sneferu begins Fourth Dynasty
